title: DATEDIF
summary: DATEDIF calculates the difference between two dates in complete years, months, or days, which makes it ideal for ages, tenure, and durations.
jobUseCases: Calculate employee tenure in years for long-service awards in HR
Work out a customer's age in completed years from their date of birth
Measure how many full months a project has been running for a status report
Compute days outstanding on an unpaid invoice for collections
Express tenure as 'X years and Y months' for an offer or appraisal letter

examples.title: Full years of service
examples.explanation: If Amit joined on 15-Mar-2019 (in B2), this returns the number of completed years between that date and today, e.g. 7. The "Y" unit counts only whole years, ignoring leftover months and days.

examples.title: Whole months between dates
examples.explanation: With a project start in B2 and end in C2, "M" returns the count of complete months between them. A span of 2-Jan to 28-Feb returns 1, because the second full month is not yet complete.

examples.title: Years plus leftover months (tenure text)
examples.explanation: The "YM" unit gives the months remaining after subtracting whole years. Combined with "Y", this builds a readable string like '7 yrs 3 mths' for Priya's service record.

examples.title: Days outstanding on an invoice
examples.explanation: With the invoice date in B2, "D" returns the total number of days elapsed until today, e.g. 42. This is a quick aging metric (note: for net-of-days you can also just use TODAY()-B2).

commonMistakes: Putting the end_date before the start_date, which causes a #NUM! error since DATEDIF needs start <= end
Using a unit other than the six valid codes ("Y","M","D","YM","YD","MD"), which returns #NUM!
Confusing "YM" (months after whole years) with "M" (total months) and getting unexpectedly large or small results
Feeding text dates like "15/03/2019" in quotes instead of real date values, causing #VALUE! errors
Relying on the documented but quirky "MD" unit, which can return wrong results across month boundaries
practiceTips: DATEDIF is a hidden function; type it fully because Excel will not autocomplete or show argument tips.
Always reference real date cells (or TODAY()) rather than typing dates as text.
Combine "Y", "YM", and "YD" with text to format human-readable durations.
Format the result cell as a number, not a date, so you see 7 instead of a stray date.
